frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly in India every man should think about prenup if possible. Aajkal marriage itself has become risky because one-sided laws are there, and after divorce the husband is treated like ATM regardless of what actually happened. I'm not saying every woman is like that, but too many cases now are about settlement and 498A pressure, so men also need some protection.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financial threats in marriage and presents legal protections like 498A and alimony as tools commonly used to exploit men. It encodes victimhood inversion, legal-system bias claims, and suspicion of women seeking financial support.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
